action.reasoning: Observation: verify.py in iteration 6 confirmed that checkpoints_v2/best.pt achieves 0.6435 accuracy and 0.4109 restricted macro-F1 on local val data, while the older checkpoints/best.pt only reaches 0.2795. The previous test eval returning ~0.029 likely evaluated the wrong checkpoint (or a corrupted file). Analysis: Before burning the remaining ~4 hours on further training, I must establish the true test macro-F1 of the good checkpoint. If test macro-F1 is in the 0.30-0.40 range, this is a strong baseline and I can invest time in training longer or refining augmentation. If it is still near random, there is a test-time bug to fix. Decision: Submit checkpoints_v2/best.pt for eval immediately. No new training command in this iteration to avoid any risk of overwriting checkpoints or resource contention.
